question:
Will the price of Ethereum be above $3,500 on November 12?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

evidences.title: What to Expect From Ethereum Price in November 2025
evidences.content: The Net Unrealized Profit/Loss (NUPL) — which gauges whether investors are sitting on profits or losses — has dipped from 0.43 to 0.39 since October 26, a 9.3% decline. That's close to the monthly low of 0.38, a level that previously triggered a 13% jump in the ETH price (from $3,750 to $4,240). Selling Incentive Reduces Selling Incentive Reduces: Glassnode This drop means investors' incentive to sell is fading, a common precursor to price stabilization. If this historical pattern holds, November could mark the point where selling pressure transitions into reaccumulation. But wait, some groups are already accumulating. ## November Could Be a Battle Between Whales and Holders While long-term holders have pulled back, whales are quietly building positions. [...] In Ethereum's case, HAR has dropped since October-end, from 31.27% to 30.45%, showing that long-term holders have slowed down accumulation and are trimming exposure instead. Holders Keep Dumping Holders Keep Dumping ETH: Glassnode This divergence suggests that whales are driving demand, while older ETH holders are trimming exposure — a dynamic that could define ETH's direction in November. As Shawn Young, Chief Analyst at...

evidences.title: Ethereum's 2025 Price Outlook: Drivers, Risks & The ...
evidences.content: ## Expert Analyst Price Predictions for 2025 Finder’s Q1 panel of 50 crypto specialists pegs ETH at $5,770 by December 2025. CoinPedia’s upside case nudges that to $5,925, while Standard Chartered sees $8,000 by 2026, implying mid‑2025 levels near $6,000 if momentum holds. On the flip side, CoinPedia’s bearish scenario warns of a retreat to $2,917 should regulatory setbacks or developer delays dent confidence, and several bank research desks place fair‑value floors near $1,500, around where we are today. Strip out the extremes and most sell‑side houses cluster around $4,000‑4,500—a 150 % gain from today yet still below 2021’s all‑time high. ## Opportunities for Ethereum Growth in 2025 ### Increasing Adoption [...] ### Regulatory Developments Europe’s MiCA “stablecoin” framework is already in place requiring any exchange servicing EU residents obtain a CASP license. This brings licensing clarity but also higher compliance costs. In the U.S., staking’s legal status remains murky even after the ETF nod, and November’s election, where U.S. President Donald Trump has vowed to make America “the crypto capital of the planet,”could swing sentiment overnight. ### DeFi And NFT Trends Ethe...

evidences.title: Ethereum ETFs Post $29.1 Million Net Inflows, Led by BlackRock, for Second Straight Day — BigGo Finance
evidences.content: Add to Google Preferred Sources U.S. spot Ethereum ETFs recorded $29.1 million in net inflows on July 2 (local time), marking a second consecutive day of positive flows. This follows the prior day's $14.92 million net inflow, which snapped a nine-day streak of net outflows. BlackRock's ETHA led the charge, pulling in $29.74 million, while VanEck and Fidelity products also saw modest inflows. In contrast, Grayscale's ETHE shed $2.75 million. While there were some positive signals—such as Fidelity's FETH flipping to net inflows after posting outflows the previous day—the concentration of capital remains pronounced, suggesting it is too early to declare a full market recovery. ##### Key Elements Ethereum ETFs Post $29.1 Million Net Inflows, Led by BlackRock, for Second Straight Day [...] U.S.-listed spot Ethereum (ETH) exchange-traded funds (ETFs) posted net inflows for a second consecutive day, signaling a gradual recovery in investor sentiment. Notably, the Ethereum ETF from BlackRock, the world's largest asset manager, strongly led the capital inflows. According to crypto data platform Trader T on July 3, U.S. spot Ethereum ETFs attracted a total of $29.1 million in net inflows...

Meanwhile, the options market paints an overall bullish story, too. Investors are paying to fence off near-term downside as puts are priced a bit richer than calls. evidences.title: BlackRock, Fidelity and six other firms receive SEC approval for ...
evidences.content: UPDATED 18:54 EDT / MAY 23 2024 ### BlackRock, Fidelity and six other firms receive SEC approval for Ethereum ETFs by Duncan Riley In a surprise decision, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission announced today that it has approved eight applications for spot Ethereum exchange-traded funds. The SEC approved 19b-4 forms — submissions made to the SEC seeking approval to list and trade a new ETF — that detailed the proposed rule changes and regulatory compliance. The eight submissions approved cover ETFs from BlackRock Inc., Fidelity, Grayscale Investments Inc., Bitwise Asset Management Inc., VanEck Associates Corp., Ark Investment Management LLC, Invesco Capital Management LLC and Franklin Templeton. [...] The SEC is approving the Ethereum ETFs now, citing improved market regulation that can help detect and deter fraudulent and manipulative acts. The SEC also said that recent court decisions and improved correlation analysis between spot and futures markets for cryptocurrencies like bitcoin influenced their decision. The SEC did note in its ruling, however, that it remains important to address investor protection concerns, such as premium/discount volatility and high managemen...

evidences.title: Sizing the Market for the Ethereum ETF | Bitcoin ETF Inflows Insight
evidences.content: to 75% after reports that securities exchanges had been contacted by the SEC. Indeed, all applications for spot Ether ETPs were approved by the SEC later that week. While we await the actual launch of these vehicles following the effectiveness of S-1 filings – which we expect to occur sometime in Summer 2024 – in this report take cues from the performance of the Bitcoin spot ETPs to make predictions about the demand that Ethereum ETPs will draw once they launch, particularly as institutions continue to expand into global markets trading for digital assets. We estimate that spot Ethereum ETPs will see approximately $5 billion in net inflows in the first five months of trading (approximately 30% of bitcoin ETP net flows). evidences.title: The SEC just approved 8 spot Ether ETFs in a landmark decision
evidences.content: Image 3 The United States Securities and Exchange Commission has approved eight spot Ether exchange-traded funds (ETFs) in the country. In a landmark decision on Thursday evening, the financial watchdog approved 19b-4 forms for the ETFs from BlackRock $BLK, Fidelity, Grayscale, Bitwise, VanEck, Ark, Invesco Galaxy, and Franklin Templeton. [...] BlackRock, Bitwise, Grayscale, Van Eck, Ark 21Shares, Fidelity, Franklin, and Invesco had previously amended their 19b-4 filings with the SEC to eliminate provisions for staking, a crypto rewards system. By doing this, these financial companies hoped to obtain approval more easily. The 19b-4 filings are documents that national exchanges like the NASDAQ or the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) submit to the SEC to request approval for listing new products on their trading platforms. #### Related Content Apple is suing OpenAI, saying it stole trade secrets Meta launched AI models to compete with OpenAI and Anthropic. The stock erased a year's worth of losses The crypto industry has welcomed the SEC’s decision, hailing it as a groundbreaking development. [...] SearchNewsletters Image 2: Logo Editions HomeLatestBusiness NewsMoney & MarketsTech &...

evidences.title: How Crypto ETFs Work: BlackRock, Grayscale, Fidelity and more
evidences.content: The Clarity Act, a crypto market structure bill, that is currently making its way through Congress seeks to clarify what is considered a commodity and what is considered a security in the US. Commodities are regulatedf by the CFTC whilst securities are regulated by the SEC. ## When Were Crypto ETFs Approved? The spot Bitcoin ETF was first approved on January 10th, 2024, by the US SEC. The approval was a momentous decision for the crypto industry after more than 10 years of rejection since the Winklevoss twins submitted the first filing for a Bitcoin ETF, the Winklevoss Bitcoin Trust, in 2013. Just 4 months after the initial Bitcoin ETF approval, the US SEC approved spot Ethereum ETFs through the acceptance of their 19b-4 filings. [...] ## Conclusion The approval of Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs fundamentally altered the flow of capital in crypto markets. The SEC's new listings framework altered the flow of capital even more, leading to the launch of may more spot and staking ETFs from a range of different digital assets. Spot crypto ETFs provide increased accessibility and regulatory legitimacy, bridging the gap between traditional finance and the digital asset space. Thanks to on-c...

evidences.title: Research Crypto & Macro Market Analysis BIT - Matrixport
evidences.content: ETH — a complete reversal from the prior weeks when ETH consistently dominated. ETH's near-end independent weakness (front backwardation, low front IV ratio at 1.194, slightly negative perp funding) is worth tracking; the 30D vol center is stable at ~1.29-1.30, so this isn't a one-way structural divergence, but if near-end ETH weakness persists it's a real underperformance signal. The one quiet warning that shouldn't get lost in the noise: long-end vol is creeping up. BTC 26MAR27 ATMF has moved W27 43.82 → W27B 44.53 → W28 45.33 — a cumulative +1.5pp over two periods. The long-end anchor that held flat through the FOMC surprise and the Iran stall is now drifting upward. Not alarming in isolation, but the direction is unambiguous. If long-end vol accelerates from here, it means the market [...] But layered against institutional options hedging and crowded retail positioning, it reads as a caution signal into resistance. If it coincides with a break in ETF inflow continuity, it upgrades to a leading indicator of retest risk. Institutions Hedge, Retail Chases — What the Derivatives Surface Is Saying Implied volatility fell as ETH rallied 8% — and that combination is diagnostic. Imp...

Why is the Fed meeting on September 17 central to the Crypto Calendar September 2025? [...] Macro catalysts rarely move in isolation. Even the cleanest CPI print or the clearest Fed signal can be overshadowed by political and geopolitical shocks. September is no exception. Alongside jobs, inflation, and rate decisions, traders also need to account for fiscal deadlines, tariff disputes, and global flashpoints that can disrupt sentiment without warning. evidences.title: Kline Candlestick Data | Binance Open Platform
evidences.content: Binance Logo Binance Logo # Kline/Candlestick Data ## API Description​ Kline/candlestick bars for a symbol. Klines are uniquely identified by their open time. Kline/Candlestick chart intervals: m -> minutes; h -> hours; d -> days; w -> weeks; M -> months ## HTTP Request​ GET `/dapi/v1/klines` `/dapi/v1/klines` ## Request Weight​ based on parameter `LIMIT` `LIMIT` | LIMIT | weight | --- | | [1,100) | 1 | | [100, 500) | 2 | | [500, 1000] | 5 | 1000 | 10 ## Request Parameters​ | Name | Type | Mandatory | Description | --- --- | | symbol | STRING | YES | After CM migration, accepts both CM and UM symbols. | | interval | ENUM | YES | | | startTime | LONG | NO | | | endTime | LONG | NO | | | limit | INT | NO | Default 500; max 1500. | [...] `startTime` `endTime` `startTime` `endTime` `limit` `endTime` `startTime` `endTime` `endTime` `startTime` `startTime` `endTime` `endTime` `endTime` `startTime` ## Response Example​ `[ [ 1591258320000, // Open time "9640.7", // Open "9642.4", // High "9640.6", // Low "9642.0", // Close (or latest price) "206", // Volume 1591258379999, // Close time "2.13660389", // Base asset volume 48, // Number of trades "119", // Taker buy volume "1.23424865", //...

evidences.title: Implied Volatility: Reading the Market's Forecast
evidences.content: The 7-day tenor is the front end and is dominated by event risk such as the next FOMC decision, CPI print, or options expiry. Front-end IV moves on the calendar rather than the regime. The 30-day tenor is the middle, where most volume sits and where market-makers concentrate, making 30D ATM a common headline measure for the surface. This piece is part of a series on how to read crypto options markets layer by layer.
